name: SamurAI Shield :: Linux apt update (Ubuntu/Debian)
description: Input contract for playbooks/linux_apt_update_with_evidence.yml (repo: prfsouto/samurai-aap-playbooks). HOSTNAMES is auto-filled by SamurAI Shield from the governed asset and hidden from the operator form.

spec.question_description: Space-separated list of Ubuntu/Debian hosts (comma also accepted). Filled automatically by SamurAI Shield from the selected asset — do not type it by hand.
Check simulates the upgrade and reports what would change. Apply performs the upgrade on the target.
all upgrades every upgradable package. security drives unattended-upgrade (the -security pocket only) and requires the unattended-upgrades package.
Only used when Update mode = all. upgrade installs new dependencies but NEVER removes a package. dist-upgrade is the full resolver and MAY remove packages.
Comma-separated package names to keep at their current version. They are put on apt-mark hold for the run; packages already held by an operator stay held.
true lets the playbook reboot the target after Apply, and only when /var/run/reboot-required is present.
summary = counts only; package = one line per apt transaction (max 300); full = complete stdout/stderr (very large logs).
Rolling batch size. 1 updates the wave strictly one host at a time.
Refuse to update when /boot has less than this much free space — a kernel upgrade with a full /boot can leave the host unbootable.
Base directory on the target where the structured evidence files are written.
